serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 300 g (one cup or tall glass), as typically served in Vietnamese-style cafes in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Avocado Coffee (Cà Phê Bơ) — estimated from USDA FoodData Central component data: Avocados, raw (FDC ID 171705), Beverages, coffee, brewed, espresso (FDC ID 174032), and Milk, condensed, sweetened (FDC ID 172217), combined in representative proportions (avocado ~40%, brewed coffee ~45%, condensed milk ~15%) per 100 g beverage.
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No direct entry for avocado coffee exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central, or FAO/INFOODS SEA databases. Values are estimated by blending USDA FoodData Central component data for avocado (raw), brewed espresso coffee, and sweetened condensed milk in proportions representative of a standard Vietnamese-style avocado coffee preparation (approximately 120 g avocado, 135 g brewed coffee, 45 g condensed milk per 300 g serving). Per-100 g values are derived from this weighted blend.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of avocado and coffee components — analogous estimate based on: Avocado GI ~10 (Atkinson FS et al., 2008, Diabetes Care); Sweetened condensed milk GI ~61 (University of Sydney GI Database); Brewed coffee GI ~0 (negligible carbohydrate). Weighted blend GI estimated at ~40 for the composite beverage.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008. Diabetes Care.
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for avocado coffee (Cà Phê Bơ). GI estimated at 40 (low) by weighted average of components: avocado GI ~10 (weight ~40%), brewed coffee GI ~1 (weight ~45%, negligible carbohydrate contribution), sweetened condensed milk GI ~61 (weight ~15%). Glycemic load per 300 g serving = (40 × 33.6 g carbohydrate) / 100 = 13.4 g. Avocado GI sourced from Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care 31:2281–2283); condensed milk GI from University of Sydney GI Database.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium content; potassium-rich avocado component
hypertension.guidance: Avocado coffee contains approximately 18 mg sodium per 100 g and provides potassium (~210 mg/100 g) from avocado, which may support blood pressure regulation; however, the condensed milk component adds sugar and saturated fat, so portion control is advised for individuals with hypertension.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Added sugar from condensed milk; moderate glycemic load per serving
type2_diabetes.guidance: Avocado coffee has an estimated GI of 40 (low) but delivers approximately 13.4 g glycemic load per standard 300 g serving due to condensed milk; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit portion size and consider requesting reduced condensed milk to lower the glycemic impact.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Predominantly monounsaturated fat from avocado; moderate saturated fat from condensed milk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The avocado component contributes heart-healthy monounsaturated fatty acids (~4.1 g/100 g), while the condensed milk adds saturated fat (~1.4 g/100 g); overall the beverage is considered low cardiovascular concern when consumed in moderate portions as part of a balanced diet.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Avocado Coffee, known in Vietnamese as Cà Phê Bơ (literally 'butter coffee'), is a popular Vietnamese-origin blended beverage made by combining ripe avocado, strong brewed Vietnamese coffee (typically robusta), sweetened condensed milk, and ice. The drink is blended or layered to create a thick, creamy, smoothie-like texture with a rich, slightly bitter coffee flavour balanced by the buttery sweetness of avocado and the caramel notes of condensed milk. It is served chilled, often in a tall glass, and is a signature item at Vietnamese cafes. In Singapore, it is offered at Vietnamese restaurants such as Co Chung Restaurant. Nutritionally, a standard 300 g serving provides approximately 324 kcal, 6.8 g fat per 100 g (predominantly monounsaturated from avocado), 11.2 g carbohydrate per 100 g (with 8.9 g sugars from condensed milk), and 1.8 g dietary fibre per 100 g. The estimated glycemic index is 40 (low), though the glycemic load per serving is moderate (~13.4 g) due to the condensed milk content. Clinically, the beverage is relatively low in sodium and provides potassium and monounsaturated fats from avocado, but individuals with Type 2 Diabetes or those managing weight should be mindful of the added sugar from condensed milk. It is vegetarian, halal-friendly, and gluten-free, but not suitable for those with dairy allergies.

confidence_reason: No direct nutritional entry for avocado coffee (Cà Phê Bơ) was found in HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central, or FAO/INFOODS SEA databases. Nutrient values are estimated by weighted component blending using USDA FoodData Central data for avocado (raw), brewed coffee, and sweetened condensed milk in representative proportions. GI is an analogous estimate based on component GI values from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008. Confidence is medium due to the estimation approach.
notes: Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central component data (avocado raw FDC 171705, brewed espresso FDC 174032, sweetened condensed milk FDC 172217) blended in proportions ~40% avocado, ~45% brewed coffee, ~15% condensed milk per 100 g beverage. Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central source data for these components. Selenium is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central source data for avocado or brewed coffee. Trans fat is 0.0 (confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central for all three components). GI is an analogous estimate of 40 (low) based on weighted component GI values; no direct GI measurement for avocado coffee exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. Glycemic load per 300 g serving = (40 × 33.6 g total carbohydrate) / 100 = 13.4 g. The food is listed under Singapore cuisine in the source field survey data but is of Vietnamese origin; cuisine is recorded as Vietnamese with serving context noted as Singapore. Price from field survey data: SGD 13.78 (single restaurant: Co Chung Restaurant, Lorong Telok). glycemic_index set to 40 (low, ≤55) based on analogous component estimate; gi_category is low per Veritas threshold rules.
